title: Handshake escrow and client-abort semantics for streaming sessions (OpenAI compatibility)
preview: Motivation: intermittent client disconnects during long-running streaming responses cause two classes of problems: 1) clients that reconnect within a short window are unable to resume because the server immediately tears down session state; 2) client-initiated cancels (or network-triggered resets) have ambiguous sem...
text: Motivation: intermittent client disconnects during long-running streaming responses cause two classes of problems: 1) clients that reconnect within a short window are unable to resume because the server immediately tears down session state; 2) client-initiated cancels (or network-triggered resets) have ambiguous semantics with respect to tool/function call idempotency and structured output flushing. This PR introduces a handshake escrow token and clarified client-abort semantics to make resume/reconnect and abort behavior deterministic and OpenAI-compatible. Key changes: - Introduce a short-lived session escrow ticket (Escrow-Token) retained server-side for a configurable grace window to support reconnect-and-resume for active stream sessions. The token is returned in initial stream response headers and via the OpenAI-compat protocol extension. - Define deterministic semantics for cli...

title: add content-disposition/metadata guards and per-tool payload quotas for multipart uploads
preview: Motivation: Tool/file attachments used by the OpenAI-compatible tool-calling surface were allowing poorly formed multipart uploads and oversized payloads which caused streaming stalls and OOMs in the serving runtime. This PR introduces explicit guards around Content-Disposition and file metadata, per-tool and per-ac...
text: Motivation: Tool/file attachments used by the OpenAI-compatible tool-calling surface were allowing poorly formed multipart uploads and oversized payloads which caused streaming stalls and OOMs in the serving runtime. This PR introduces explicit guards around Content-Disposition and file metadata, per-tool and per-account payload quotas for multipart/form-data parts, improved multipart boundary handling to avoid partial-read hangs in stream mode, and small compatibility tweaks so the OpenAI compatibility layer surfaces a stable `files` metadata object for tool calls. Changes are intentionally conservative: they add validation and soft-failure modes (400 + diagnostics) by default and an env flag to opt into hard rejection for stricter enterprise deployments. The description below summarizes goals, changes, test coverage, and rollout plan.

title: Hybrid comm/compute: staggered NCCL streams + dynamic kernel placement for tensor-parallel workloads
preview: Motivation: For TP models we observed tail latency spikes when large collective operations serialized across the default NCCL stream and blocked compute kernels on subsequent micro-batches. This PR implements a hybrid comm/compute execution strategy: staggered NCCL comm streams per tensor-parallel group, opportunist...
text:
Motivation: For TP models we observed tail latency spikes when large collective operations serialized across the default NCCL stream and blocked compute kernels on subsequent micro-batches. This PR implements a hybrid comm/compute execution strategy: staggered NCCL comm streams per tensor-parallel group, opportunistic kernel placement on secondary compute streams, and a lightweight dynamic placement heuristic that routes small-kernel work to a dedicated low-latency stream to maximize overlap without increasing memory pressure.

Summary of changes: implemented a per-TP-group stream allocator that staggers all-reduce/all-gather launches across NCCL streams (comm_stream_0..N) to reduce contention; added a kernel placement layer in the runtime scheduler that classifies kernels by inferred latency (fast vs slow) and assigns them to either the main compute stream or a fast-path microbatch s...

title: compat: strict media negotiation + per-file ingestion-rate-limits for tool attachments
preview: Context: Our OpenAI-compat layer has had recurring issues when clients upload ambiguous or binary attachments without clear content-type or length headers. These cause parsing retries, confused streaming boundaries, and occasional function_call failures when tool runtimes receive corrupted payloads. This PR introduc...
text:
Context: Our OpenAI-compat layer has had recurring issues when clients upload ambiguous or binary attachments without clear content-type or length headers. These cause parsing retries, confused streaming boundaries, and occasional function_call failures when tool runtimes receive corrupted payloads. This PR introduces two complementary protections: (1) deterministic media negotiation and normalization so the compat layer always annotates attachments with a canonical content-type + checksum; (2) per-file ingestion rate limits and adaptive chunk checks to prevent slow/bursty clients from overwhelming streaming parsers and downstream tool runtimes.

What this changes (high level):
- Compat layer now enforces an Accept/Content-Type negotiation step for incoming attachments. Preferred content-types for tool calls are application/json (pointer/meta), application/octet-stream (binary payload...

title: v1/chat: stabilize streaming handshake; enforce stop-token precedence and introduce role-alias mapping
preview: Context: Customers integrating via the OpenAI /v1/chat.completions surface have seen two classes of issues in production: 1) transient stream disconnects during tool/function call handoffs (client resumes see inconsistent framing), and 2) ambiguity in how stop tokens are applied when stop arrays are provided alongsi...
text: Context: Customers integrating via the OpenAI /v1/chat.completions surface have seen two classes of issues in production: 1) transient stream disconnects during tool/function call handoffs (client resumes see inconsistent framing), and 2) ambiguity in how stop tokens are applied when stop arrays are provided alongside role-level stop hints or tool-generated token sequences. This PR unifies several compatibility behaviors to reduce surprise for downstream clients and to harden streaming handshakes during function/tool call routing. Motivation: Provide predictable, backward-compatible semantics for v1 chat parity while reducing the failure surface during streaming toolcalls. Goals: (a) stabilize the streaming handshake so that tool/function call initiation and the subsequent resumption of the assistant stream never produce overlapping or dropped deltas, (b) define and implement a determ...

title: Optimize INT8 dequant-fusion kernel and smoothquant scheduling to reduce p99 and token cost
preview: Motivation: For the quantized inference stack we've been shipping two main bottlenecks: (1) the dequantization+matmul fusion path for INT8 kernels introduces extra memory traffic and kernel launches at each attention and FFN block; (2) SmoothQuant scaling interacts poorly with our scheduler and continuous-batching h...
text: Motivation: For the quantized inference stack we've been shipping two main bottlenecks: (1) the dequantization+matmul fusion path for INT8 kernels introduces extra memory traffic and kernel launches at each attention and FFN block; (2) SmoothQuant scaling interacts poorly with our scheduler and continuous-batching heuristics, causing variable p99 regressions under mixed-sequence workloads. This PR introduces three coordinated changes: 1) a fused dequantize+gemm kernel that accepts packed INT8 weights and produces FP16 accumulation with optional requantization hooks; 2) a scheduling tweak that biases smoothquant scaling application to the slower stage of the pipeline and batches compatible sequence shapes together more aggressively; 3) a lightweight KV-cache-aware fast path that skips per-step dequant for cached KV blocks and reuses FP16 cached keys when the model quantization profile ...

title: hybrid-staged-canary-controller-with-statistical-gates-and-fallthrough-routing
preview: Motivation: enable safer multi-stage experiments and automated rollback by introducing a hybrid staged canary controller that combines time/traffic phasing, Bayesian statistical gates, and deterministic fallthrough routing. This PR implements a controller that can run A/B tests, shadow trials, and progressive canari...
text: Motivation: enable safer multi-stage experiments and automated rollback by introducing a hybrid staged canary controller that combines time/traffic phasing, Bayesian statistical gates, and deterministic fallthrough routing. This PR implements a controller that can run A/B tests, shadow trials, and progressive canaries using a configurable stability window, significance threshold, and quarantine queue. Key features: 1) BayesianSignificanceEngine (configurable priors) to decide pass/fail for metric families; 2) HealthSnapshot store that captures per-route histograms and model KV cache hit rates at sample intervals; 3) WeightDecaySchedule to gradually reduce canary traffic on persistent regressions before triggering full rollback; 4) FallthroughRouting that routes traffic to a fallback model variant instantly on hard failures while preserving request affinity; 5) QuarantineQueue and auto...

title: fragmented-kernel-workspace-pool-kv-rolling-reserve-and-adaptive-batching-prioritizer
preview: Motivation: Under heavy continuous-batching workloads we observe fragmented temporary workspace allocations and conservative KV cache reservations that inflate peak GPU memory by ~15-25%. This PR introduces a kernel-fragment pool allocator, a compacting fragment coalescer, a KV rolling-reserve policy with age-based ...
text: Motivation: Under heavy continuous-batching workloads we observe fragmented temporary workspace allocations and conservative KV cache reservations that inflate peak GPU memory by ~15-25%. This PR introduces a kernel-fragment pool allocator, a compacting fragment coalescer, a KV rolling-reserve policy with age-based demotion, and an adaptive batching prioritizer that protects tail latency while increasing packing efficiency. Goals: 1) reduce peak transient workspace (scratch) footprint, 2) lower KV cache reserved overhead for workloads with skewed hotsets, 3) keep 95p/99p latency equal or improved under steady-state continuous batching. Changes (high level): - runtime/worker: implement FragmentPool allocator that suballocates kernel workspace into variable-length fragments mapped to kernel affinity classes; supports fast reuse and best-fit coalescing on free. - runtime/scheduler: add A...

title: tokenized-fairness-scaler-and-util-gap-reporter
preview: Context and motivation: customers on Dedicated have asked for better fairness when bursts co-exist with steady throughput tenants. Existing burst pools and reservations ensure throughput but can starve latency-sensitive tenants during microbursts. This PR implements a tokenized fairness scaler: a lightweight token a...
text:
Context and motivation: customers on Dedicated have asked for better fairness when bursts co-exist with steady throughput tenants. Existing burst pools and reservations ensure throughput but can starve latency-sensitive tenants during microbursts. This PR implements a tokenized fairness scaler: a lightweight token accounting layer that mints per-tenant 'burst tokens' proportional to committed throughput and dynamically redistributes spare tokens based on queue-latency buckets.

Design summary: the scaler observes per-tenant request queue latency percentiles (p50/p95/p99) and token consumption rate. When a tenant's p95 exceeds its latency target and tokens are available in the cluster pool, the allocator grants ephemeral tokens that raise the tenant's concurrency cap for short windows (configurable TTL). Tokens are reclaimed proactively when latency recovers or when they expire. Token ...

title: per-layer kernel occupancy probes + adaptive batch-window tracer (KV-stamp profiler)
preview: Summary: Instrumentation and microbench additions to help diagnose layer-level kernel occupancy and batch-window interactions with KV-cache. This PR introduces a lightweight per-layer occupancy probe, a stamp-based KV-cache sampler ("kv-stamp profiler"), and an adaptive batch-window tracer that records contiguous re...
text:
Summary: Instrumentation and microbench additions to help diagnose layer-level kernel occupancy and batch-window interactions with KV-cache. This PR introduces a lightweight per-layer occupancy probe, a stamp-based KV-cache sampler ("kv-stamp profiler"), and an adaptive batch-window tracer that records contiguous request grouping behavior used by the continuous-batching scheduler. All probes are zero-cost when disabled and are guarded behind runtime flags and RBAC-controlled debug endpoints.

Motivation and context: We observed tail-latency regressions under mixed request shapes where a few large layers dominated GPU occupancy and caused scheduler-induced head-of-line blocking. Existing coarse counters were insufficient to map slowdowns to individual kernel launches or to correlate KV-cache reuse with batch-window size. This work aims to give engineers the tools to: (1) attribute occu...

title: add-inference-health-probe-and-sampled-kernel-queue-explorer
preview: Adds a lightweight inference health probe and a sampled kernel-queue explorer to improve incident triage and long-tail cost attribution. Motivation: recurring short-lived kernel queue stalls were noisy in SLO pages and made it hard to trace whether slow tails were caused by model-level token spikes, kernel contentio...
text: Adds a lightweight inference health probe and a sampled kernel-queue explorer to improve incident triage and long-tail cost attribution. Motivation: recurring short-lived kernel queue stalls were noisy in SLO pages and made it hard to trace whether slow tails were caused by model-level token spikes, kernel contention, or tenant-induced backpressure. This PR introduces: 1) inference_health_probe: periodic synthetic pings that exercise a small, deterministic token path and emit a health metric (inference_probe_latency_ms) plus an opaque probe_id for trace linking; 2) sampled_kernel_queue_explorer: a new trace sampler that, when a probe or real invocation exceeds a kernel-queue latency threshold, captures a compact kernel-queue snapshot and stores a compacted span with queue_depth, head_kernel_id, and estimated_token_cost_hint; 3) dashboard panels: a compact 'Probe Pulse' timeseries, ker...

title: Graceful disconnect: buffered tool-call retry and reconnect window for streaming API
preview: Motivation: We observed customer incidents where short-lived client disconnects during multi-step function/tool-calling flows caused duplicate tool invocations or orphaned streaming sessions, leading to higher costs and inconsistent structured outputs. This PR introduces a server-side in-flight buffer and a short re...
text: Motivation: We observed customer incidents where short-lived client disconnects during multi-step function/tool-calling flows caused duplicate tool invocations or orphaned streaming sessions, leading to higher costs and inconsistent structured outputs. This PR introduces a server-side in-flight buffer and a short reconnect window with idempotency reconciliation to preserve end-to-end semantics while keeping latency and cost in check. Summary of changes: 1) Session manager: add a buffered-inflight queue that retains in-progress tool invocations for a configurable reconnect window (default 20s). Sessions now emit a compact session token (reconnect_token) on streaming start. 2) Tool invocation idempotency: introduce X-Tool-Invocation-Id header and runtime-side idempotency table so duplicate replays are deduplicated; tools should return idempotency-safe responses. 3) Reconnect/reconcile f...

title: Topology-aware peer balancing, async kernel dispatch, and KV-cache coalescing
preview: Motivation: Multi-GPU tensor-parallel workloads suffer from suboptimal collective placement and small-KV churn when requests are micro-batched across heterogeneous racks. This PR implements three coordinated runtime optimizations to improve throughput and tail latency: (1) topology-aware peer balancing: build NVLink...
text: Motivation: Multi-GPU tensor-parallel workloads suffer from suboptimal collective placement and small-KV churn when requests are micro-batched across heterogeneous racks. This PR implements three coordinated runtime optimizations to improve throughput and tail latency: (1) topology-aware peer balancing: build NVLink/NIC-aware ring/graph membership using a lightweight port-speed probe so collectives prefer intra-node/NVLink lanes and minimize cross-node hops, and dynamically rebalance peer pairs when per-step bandwidth estimates indicate hotlinks; (2) async kernel dispatch: move per-layer kernel launch to a dispatch thread pair that annotates CUDA streams with priority and orders kernels for overlap with NCCL comms, and add a non-blocking enqueue-locality check so compute work can start as soon as required dependencies are queued; (3) KV-cache coalescing at the request router: group co...

title: Smoother autoscaler signal and gateway backpressure for dedicated pools
preview: Summary: Introduces a smoothing layer in the Dedicated Autoscaler and a lightweight backpressure signal propagated from the gateway to fleet controllers. This reduces autoscaler thrash on spiky traffic and gives gateways a controlled way to shed load gracefully against per-pool soft capacity and throughput guarantee...
text:
Summary: Introduces a smoothing layer in the Dedicated Autoscaler and a lightweight backpressure signal propagated from the gateway to fleet controllers. This reduces autoscaler thrash on spiky traffic and gives gateways a controlled way to shed load gracefully against per-pool soft capacity and throughput guarantees.

Motivation: Customers on Dedicated have reported oscillations when short bursts cross autoscaler thresholds and then recede; this leads to unnecessary GPU spin-ups and billing variance, as well as transient error spikes. The gateway currently responds with 429s or fallback heuristics; we need a first-class, observable mechanism that cooperates with autoscaling and preserves headroom for SLA-critical requests.

Changes in this PR:
- Autoscaler: add Exponential Moving Average (EMA) smoothing of utilization and request rate with configurable half-life per-pool. New config ...

title: Resumable streaming: disconnect quarantine, server timeouts, and function-call idempotency
preview: Context and motivation: Production customers hit a class of errors where brief client disconnects (mobile network flaps, browser tab swaps) caused long-running streaming requests that invoked tools to either get duplicated or be lost after client reconnect. Existing resume tokens preserved raw streaming offsets but ...
text:
Context and motivation: Production customers hit a class of errors where brief client disconnects (mobile network flaps, browser tab swaps) caused long-running streaming requests that invoked tools to either get duplicated or be lost after client reconnect. Existing resume tokens preserved raw streaming offsets but did not maintain function-call intent or partial outputs, making resuming tool invocations unsafe. This PR introduces a small set of changes to make streaming resumes predictable and safe for function/tool calls and structured output in the OpenAI compatibility layer.

What this change does (single continuous description): Adds a server-side "reconnect quarantine" (default 20s) during which a session can reconnect and claim in-flight stream state; introduces a server-enforced soft-cancel TTL for tool/function calls that allows the runtime to decide whether to allow the tool...

title: Introduce Toolcall Conductor with Async Signal Latching and OpenAI-compatible Result Hydration
preview: Context: Customers increasingly run multi-tool workflows where the model issues parallel function/tool calls and expects deterministic result composition, streaming stability, and graceful retries. Motivation: previous ad-hoc orchestration led to gibberish structured outputs, duplicate side-effects when retries happ...
text: Context: Customers increasingly run multi-tool workflows where the model issues parallel function/tool calls and expects deterministic result composition, streaming stability, and graceful retries. Motivation: previous ad-hoc orchestration led to gibberish structured outputs, duplicate side-effects when retries happened, and brittle OpenAI-compat behavior for function-calling sequences. This PR introduces a new lightweight Toolcall Conductor that centralizes execution ergonomics: async signal latching to coordinate parallel tool runs, idempotent result hydration to prevent duplicate injections, and an OpenAI-compat shim for function-calling semantics in streamed responses. Summary of changes: 1) runtime: add redwood/pkg/runtime/toolcall/conductor.go implementing a conductor that tracks call IDs, latches result signals, and emits ordered hydration events to the streaming layer. 2) api:...

title: Policy-driven gradient rollouts and shadow/A-B traffic orchestration
preview: Motivation: provide a single policy layer that can express canary gradients, A/B splits, shadowing, and automated rollback triggers for model variant rollouts across Hosted, Dedicated, and Private deployments. This PR implements a policy-driven rollout engine and runtime hooks to route traffic incrementally, evaluat...
text:
Motivation: provide a single policy layer that can express canary gradients, A/B splits, shadowing, and automated rollback triggers for model variant rollouts across Hosted, Dedicated, and Private deployments. This PR implements a policy-driven rollout engine and runtime hooks to route traffic incrementally, evaluate health metrics, and trigger automated rollback or promotion when thresholds are met.

What this change contains: checklist: [x] rollout policy DSL and parser; [x] runtime router integration (routing-by-policy); [x] metrics adapter to evaluate success criteria (latency, error-rate, quality-regression hooks); [x] shadow mode implementation (non-impacting mirrored requests); [x] A/B split support with weighted sampling; [x] automated rollback circuit with hysteresis and cooldown; [x] console integration for visualizing rollout state and editing policies; [x] e2e and unit tes...

title: Multiplexer: parallel tool execution, idempotent retries, and ergonomic result injection
preview: Motivation: production workloads increasingly issue multiple tool calls per response (lookups, enrichment, external actions) and clients expect predictable injection semantics, low-latency parallelism, and transparent retry behavior when external tools fail. This PR introduces a lightweight multiplexer for tool exec...
text:
Motivation: production workloads increasingly issue multiple tool calls per response (lookups, enrichment, external actions) and clients expect predictable injection semantics, low-latency parallelism, and transparent retry behavior when external tools fail. This PR introduces a lightweight multiplexer for tool execution that: 1) executes independent tool calls in parallel when safe, 2) exposes an explicit idempotency token + retry middleware for transient tool failures, 3) provides an ergonomic result-injection API surface for handlers (deterministic injection points with ordering hints), and 4) keeps OpenAI-compatibility behaviors (function-calling fallback and structured outputs) intact.

Summary of changes: commits implement a runtime-level ToolMultiplexer that accepts a list of ToolCall requests and returns deterministic ToolResults with stable ids and insertion points. We added ...

title: bake signed artifact index and provenance into private-installer release manifests
preview: Motivation: private deployments (VPC/on-prem) require a reproducible, signed index that bundles release artifacts (installer tarballs, Helm charts, Terraform modules), attestations (SBOM/prov.json), and signature metadata so customers can verify what they deploy. This PR introduces a baked 'signed-artifact-index' ge...
text: Motivation: private deployments (VPC/on-prem) require a reproducible, signed index that bundles release artifacts (installer tarballs, Helm charts, Terraform modules), attestations (SBOM/prov.json), and signature metadata so customers can verify what they deploy. This PR introduces a baked 'signed-artifact-index' generation step in the private-installer release pipeline, integrates Notary v2-compatible signing (via GCP KMS in CI), surfaces provenance files alongside Helm and Terraform artifacts, and wires the installer/upgrade tooling to consume the index during installs and rollbacks. Implementation summary: 1) CI: add .github/workflows/release-private-installer-index.yml that builds artifacts in a hermetic container, generates SBOMs (syft), produces provenance JSON, and invokes notary-v2 client to produce signatures and an OCI index blob that contains pointers to signed artifacts; 2...

title: v1/chat.completions: resilient stream-fragmenting + truncated-logprobs + toolcall backoff
preview: Motivation: We've observed two classes of production failures in the OpenAI /v1/chat.completions compatibility adapter when tool/function calling and long-running model responses are involved: 1) clients receiving partial or duplicated streamed chunks after transient network blips or internal retries, and 2) malform...
text:
Motivation: We've observed two classes of production failures in the OpenAI /v1/chat.completions compatibility adapter when tool/function calling and long-running model responses are involved: 1) clients receiving partial or duplicated streamed chunks after transient network blips or internal retries, and 2) malformed structured outputs (JSON) emitted incrementally by the model which break downstream tool parsing. Additionally, customers running toolcall heavy workloads saw elevated tail error rates when the function invocation subsystem experienced transient 5xxs or long latencies.

This PR implements a set of protocol and runtime changes to improve UX and parity with typical OpenAI semantics while keeping streaming stable:
- Stream fragment reconciliation: attach a "stream_fragment_id" UUID to every top-level event in the stream envelope and an optional "resume_token" when resuming....

title: dynamic per-channel requant + KV-aware jitter-resilient continuous-batching
preview: Background and motivation: Production workloads show variance in per-channel activation distributions across prompts and token positions which makes static requant scales suboptimal. We observed systematic p99 latency spikes when the runtime switched between hot and cold KV cache footprints under continuous-batching...
text: Background and motivation: Production workloads show variance in per-channel activation distributions across prompts and token positions which makes static requant scales suboptimal. We observed systematic p99 latency spikes when the runtime switched between hot and cold KV cache footprints under continuous-batching; these were exacerbated by small batching jitter when multiple streams aligned. This PR implements a dynamic per-channel requant stage combined with a KV-cache-aware scheduling hint and a jitter-resilient continuous-batching policy to smooth throughput while keeping low tail latency. Scope: 1) A lightweight per-channel scale estimator that runs on the microkernel hot path and updates requant multipliers at configurable amortization windows. 2) A cheap, lock-free KV cache sizing hint that the scheduler uses to avoid abrupt prefix evictions during batch coalescing. 3) A jitt...

title: hybrid flashattn + lazy-kv-eviction + operator-fusion for long-seq workloads
preview: Motivation: Production workloads with mixed short- and long-context requests show uneven tail latency and high aggregate token cost due to conservative KV cache retention and generic attention kernel selection. This PR introduces a hybrid attention path that (1) dispatches FlashAttention when safe, (2) uses a lightw...
text:
Motivation: Production workloads with mixed short- and long-context requests show uneven tail latency and high aggregate token cost due to conservative KV cache retention and generic attention kernel selection. This PR introduces a hybrid attention path that (1) dispatches FlashAttention when safe, (2) uses a lightweight fused matmul-add-dropout kernel for mid-sized heads, and (3) falls back to the existing fallback kernel for pathological shapes. In parallel, we implement a lazy KV eviction policy that decouples KV entry lifetime from request completion and enables opportunistic eviction under memory pressure. Together these changes reduce long-sequence p99 latency and improve throughput for streaming workloads while keeping memory bounded.

What changed (high level): implemented a Flash-aware scheduler that picks kernel variant at invocation time based on sequence length, head size,...

title: Introduce token-lifecycle burnup histogram, cross-service latency fingerprint, and alert coalescer
preview: Context: token-level SLO noise and long-tail cost/latency alerts are creating alert fatigue. Existing token-cost rollups capture per-token aggregates but struggle to: (1) show how cost accumulates across pipeline stages (ingest, model, postprocess), (2) identify cross-service latency shape changes (same total latenc...
text:
Context: token-level SLO noise and long-tail cost/latency alerts are creating alert fatigue. Existing token-cost rollups capture per-token aggregates but struggle to: (1) show how cost accumulates across pipeline stages (ingest, model, postprocess), (2) identify cross-service latency shape changes (same total latency but different phase breakdown), and (3) suppress repeated similar alerts that indicate the same incident.

What this PR does: introduces three coordinated pieces:
1) token_lifecycle_burnup histogram metric (metric/token_lifecycle_burnup): records cumulative token cost/latency at stage boundaries per invocation (labels: tenant_id, model, route, stage). Supports configurable buckets and exported rollups.
2) latency_fingerprint: a small vectorized signature (string) derived from percentile deltas across stages for an invocation; stored as a tag on traces and emitted as a low...

title: Improve chat stream framing, function-call handshake, and logprob envelope
preview: Motivation: We observed intermittent corruption of streamed messages when function calls were emitted mid-chunk and when stop sequences split across buffer boundaries. Consumers (third-party SDKs and internal examples) reported mis-attributed logprobs and truncated function-call payloads. This causes downstream tool...
text:
Motivation: We observed intermittent corruption of streamed messages when function calls were emitted mid-chunk and when stop sequences split across buffer boundaries. Consumers (third-party SDKs and internal examples) reported mis-attributed logprobs and truncated function-call payloads. This causes downstream tooling that relies on deterministic final outputs and token-level diagnostics to misbehave.

Changes in this PR: added a lightweight framing state machine to the streaming runtime so every SSE/data chunk is annotated with a stable boundary token and an explicit event for function-call lifecycle: 'fn_call.start' and 'fn_call.end'. This removes ambiguity about when a tool invocation begins and ends across stream re-fragments. To surface token-level diagnostics without polluting the incremental stream we emit a final 'logprob_envelope' JSON object as the last event for both strea...

title: typed errors + ergonomic iterable reconnect API across SDKs
preview: Motivation: Developers repeatedly hit vague runtime errors when streams failed (auth handshake, transient network) and had to implement ad-hoc parsing logic to decide whether to retry, resume, or surface an error. This PR introduces language-native typed error classes across the three SDKs (Python/TypeScript/Go) and...
text:
Motivation: Developers repeatedly hit vague runtime errors when streams failed (auth handshake, transient network) and had to implement ad-hoc parsing logic to decide whether to retry, resume, or surface an error. This PR introduces language-native typed error classes across the three SDKs (Python/TypeScript/Go) and a unified ergonomic iterable reconnect API for stream consumers.

What this change does: 
- Introduces TypedError/StreamError subclasses with machine-friendly error_code, retriable, and suggestion fields across SDKs.
- Adds a new StreamIterable API (python: StreamIterable, ts: createStreamIterable, go: StreamIterator) that yields typed result objects and automatically attempts a single reconnect on retriable failures by default with backoff tuning. Reconnect behavior is configurable by the caller.
- Deprecates low-level sync callback hooks (on_chunk/on_error) in favor of a...

title: concurrent-tool-runner: parallel tool invocation + OpenAI function_call compatibility updates
preview: Motivation: customers increasingly chain multiple tool calls per turn and want lower tail latency by running compatible tools in parallel, plus predictable result injection and idempotent retries. This PR introduces a server-side concurrent tool runner, extends the OpenAI-compatible function_call surface to allow ex...
text: Motivation: customers increasingly chain multiple tool calls per turn and want lower tail latency by running compatible tools in parallel, plus predictable result injection and idempotent retries. This PR introduces a server-side concurrent tool runner, extends the OpenAI-compatible function_call surface to allow explicit parallel calls and tool-result references, and updates SDK helpers and streaming merge semantics to keep the API stable for existing clients. Summary of changes: 1) runtime: add services/inference/tool_runner/concurrent_runner with a pluggable execution strategy (sequential | parallel | hybrid), a run_id-based idempotency layer for retries, and deterministic merge ordering for structured output injection. 2) api/openai-compat: extend function_call payload with optional keys: "parallel_calls" (array of tool names), "tool_result_refs" (map-like list of result identifie...

title: Add DMA stall & kernel-latency counters with sigprof-compatible timeline exporter
preview: Context: We need finer-grained, low-overhead indicators of where inference stalls occur inside the serving runtime so that model routing, batching and kernel-selection heuristics can be tuned without attaching heavy profilers. This PR adds DMA-stall counters collected from the GPU driver telemetry shim, per-kernel l...
text:
Context: We need finer-grained, low-overhead indicators of where inference stalls occur inside the serving runtime so that model routing, batching and kernel-selection heuristics can be tuned without attaching heavy profilers. This PR adds DMA-stall counters collected from the GPU driver telemetry shim, per-kernel latency histograms sampled into buckets, and a compact timeline exporter that emits SigProf-compatible records that downstream dashboards (and our internal perfboard) can ingest. Motivation: several customer incidents pointed to intermittent pauses that didn't show up in token-level latency traces (ENG-1223). Existing CPU-side sampling catches high-level stalls but misses kernel queuing and DMA scheduling hotspots.

Summary of changes (high level):
- runtime/telemetry: new dma_counters.{h,cc} implementing low-overhead atomic counters for DMA queue stalls and preemption event...

title: Capacity-aware SLO relaxation with regional soft-eviction safeguard
preview: Context: We saw repeated cascade evictions during multi-region capacity spikes where strict SLO enforcement triggered aggressive circuit breakers, causing higher tail latency and customer throttles (SUP-9812). This PR introduces a capacity-aware, timebound SLO relaxation layer plus a regional soft-eviction safeguard...
text: Context: We saw repeated cascade evictions during multi-region capacity spikes where strict SLO enforcement triggered aggressive circuit breakers, causing higher tail latency and customer throttles (SUP-9812). This PR introduces a capacity-aware, timebound SLO relaxation layer plus a regional soft-eviction safeguard that stages evictions to avoid synchronized mass-drains. What changed: 1) control/allocator: add 'capacity-aware slo_relaxation' policy that dynamically relaxes non-critical SLOs by a configurable percentage when sustained node-utilization exceeds a threshold for a hold window; 2) routing/eviction: introduce 'soft-eviction' state where instances are marked for probabilistic eviction over a decay window instead of immediate hard drain, enabling load to bleed off while respecting per-tenant priority buckets; 3) telemetry: new metrics (slo_relaxation.active, soft_eviction.pen...

title: introduce-server-timebox-and-idempotent-cancel-fallbacks-for-streaming-compat
preview: Context: production customers observed cases where long lived streaming sessions (tool calls + structured output) were left in indeterminate states after a client network disconnect or a client-initiated Cancel. Motivation: we need a predictable server-side safety net that bounds resource residency time, ensures too...
text: Context: production customers observed cases where long lived streaming sessions (tool calls + structured output) were left in indeterminate states after a client network disconnect or a client-initiated Cancel. Motivation: we need a predictable server-side safety net that bounds resource residency time, ensures tool-call idempotency when retries/resume occur, and provides a clear fallback path (trimmed response + final event) that OpenAI-compatible clients can rely on. This PR implements a server-side streaming timebox (configurable per-route) that gracefully converts oversized/inflight streams into a final 'timeboxed' terminal event, augments client-cancel handling to be idempotent across reconnect/resume windows, and plumbs a compact resume token that includes tool-call idempotency metadata. Summary of changes: 1) stream manager: add configurable timebox_ms and a deterministic fina...

title: multi-tool-execution: ergonomic API + parallel tool runner + result injection + retry policies
preview: Motivation: Improve ergonomics and reliability for tool/function-calling in the OpenAI-compat layer and native Redwood API. Customers asked for easy multi-tool invocations, deterministic injection of tool results into continuations, safe parallel tool execution, and more robust retry/backoff for transient tool failu...
text: Motivation: Improve ergonomics and reliability for tool/function-calling in the OpenAI-compat layer and native Redwood API. Customers asked for easy multi-tool invocations, deterministic injection of tool results into continuations, safe parallel tool execution, and more robust retry/backoff for transient tool failures. This PR introduces a small, backwards-compatible API extension plus a new parallel tool-runner implementation and runtime wiring for result injection and structured output hints. Summary of changes: 1) API: add `tools` invocation signature that supports multiple tool calls in a single step and an optional `parallel` flag. 2) Runtime: implement ParallelToolRunner with optimistic concurrency, coordinated token streaming, and ordered result injection points. 3) Retry semantics: add configurable retry/backoff policies per-tool with circuit-breaking thresholds and idempoten...

title: dynamic per-route payload limits + streaming proxy buffer tuning to stabilize function-calls and OpenAI-compatible chunking
preview: Motivation: Several production routes (chat-stream, tools/function_call, and OpenAI-compat proxying) have experienced intermittent stalls and truncated function_call payloads when large intermediate tool outputs are streamed in small chunks. Root cause summary: a) statically-sized streaming proxy flush thresholds an...
text:
Motivation: Several production routes (chat-stream, tools/function_call, and OpenAI-compat proxying) have experienced intermittent stalls and truncated function_call payloads when large intermediate tool outputs are streamed in small chunks. Root cause summary: a) statically-sized streaming proxy flush thresholds and buffer headroom do not match per-route payload patterns; b) global payload size limit was too coarse (caused safe-failures for tool outputs but allowed oversized non-streamed requests); c) OpenAI compatibility shim assumed final-message framing which breaks when intermediate buffer flushes split function_call JSON tokens across frames.

What this PR does (summary): Adds a dynamic per-route payload limiter and adaptive streaming-proxy buffer tuning. The gateway now derives two parameters per route: effective_payload_limit (bytes) and buffer_flush_threshold (bytes). Derivat...

title: Introduce 'Thrifty' inference profile generator for conservative batching/caching/quant presets
preview: Adds a new service+CLI to synthesize conservative, production-safe default inference profiles from short trace samples. Motivation: many customers hesitate to enable aggressive batching/quantization/caching because they lack templates that balance unit-economics wins with latency/regression risk. This change introdu...
text: Adds a new service+CLI to synthesize conservative, production-safe default inference profiles from short trace samples. Motivation: many customers hesitate to enable aggressive batching/quantization/caching because they lack templates that balance unit-economics wins with latency/regression risk. This change introduces 'thrifty' profiles that err on the side of lower cost while preserving tail-latency SLOs. Key pieces: 1) trace-sampler: lightweight offline analyzer that ingests representative request traces and outputs distribution summaries (seq len, token mix, prefix reuse). 2) profile-generator: rules engine that maps trace summaries to a prioritized preset (batch size, prefetch window, prefix cache policy, int8/4 quantization candidate with safety fences). 3) rw-profile-gen CLI and Docker image for customers to run locally or in infra pipelines. 4) integration with recommendations...

title: Fold per-layer scale into inline requantizer; attention KV-cache compatibility
preview: Motivation: INT8 and FP8 paths pay a per-token cost for explicit scale/dequant round-trips in the attention kernel. This PR folds stable per-layer/chunk scaling factors into an inline requantizer inside the GEMM/attention kernels and adds compatibility glue for our attention KV-cache (so cached keys/values don't tri...
text: Motivation: INT8 and FP8 paths pay a per-token cost for explicit scale/dequant round-trips in the attention kernel. This PR folds stable per-layer/chunk scaling factors into an inline requantizer inside the GEMM/attention kernels and adds compatibility glue for our attention KV-cache (so cached keys/values don't trigger extra conversions on read). Goals: reduce per-token arithmetic, improve single-stream latency, and keep cached tensors bit-compatible across quant/float fallbacks. Implementation summary: 1) introduce a lightweight FoldedScale descriptor that is emitted at model-load time and stored alongside quant metadata; 2) implement inline_requantizer.{cc,hh} which applies scale folding + rounding once on kernel entry and keeps activations in the requantized layout for the kernel duration; 3) modify attention KV load/store paths to check for FoldedScale and avoid redundant dequant...

title: Instrument kernel-level latency fingerprints and introduce adaptive kernel-fingerprint alerting
preview: Motivation: We see occasional long-tail invocation latency where root cause is GPU kernel scheduling/serialization rather than model token path. Existing observability surfaces capture per-invocation token timing but lack a compact way to correlate GPU kernel-level skew across tenants and hardware. This PR instrumen...
text:
Motivation: We see occasional long-tail invocation latency where root cause is GPU kernel scheduling/serialization rather than model token path. Existing observability surfaces capture per-invocation token timing but lack a compact way to correlate GPU kernel-level skew across tenants and hardware. This PR instruments low-level kernel timing as compact 'fingerprints', surfaces a kernel heatmap dashboard, and introduces an adaptive alert that suppresses noise during autoscale events.

What this PR does (high-level):
- Adds runtime span attributes: kernel_name, kernel_ms, gpu_sm_id, kernel_queue_depth. Spans are emitted by the serving runtime when kernels are dispatched.
- Emits a new metric series: telemetry.kernel_fingerprint with labels {model_family, hw_class, kernel_name, tenant_id? (opt)}, using reservoir sampling to limit cardinality.
- Adds a Kernel Heatmap dashboard (dashboards...

title: v1/chat: output coordinator + role-pruner for compact structured tool responses
preview: Motivation: customers relying on OpenAI /v1/chat.completions compatibility have increasingly complex tool/function outputs and varied role labels from legacy clients. The streaming path was producing verbose, repeated JSON fragments for tool responses and leaking low-confidence token-level logprobs during long strea...
text:
Motivation: customers relying on OpenAI /v1/chat.completions compatibility have increasingly complex tool/function outputs and varied role labels from legacy clients. The streaming path was producing verbose, repeated JSON fragments for tool responses and leaking low-confidence token-level logprobs during long streams. This PR introduces an 'output coordinator' layer in the v1 chat adapter and a conservative 'role-pruner' to normalize incoming roles and emit a compact structured mode for tool/function outputs.

Summary of changes: added an adapter-level coordinator that: 1) recognizes and collapses repeated tool JSON fragments into a single compact structure when clients request compact output (new query param compact_output=true), 2) supplies a configurable stop_hint_priority so that explicit stop sequences sent by the API call are respected ahead of model-internal heuristics when de...

title: generate delta upgrade index and SBOM stamping for private-installer CI
preview: This PR adds a CI-first pipeline to produce deterministic delta upgrade packages and stamp SBOM/provenance metadata into the private-installer release artifacts. Motivation: customers running Private (VPC/on-prem) need compact upgrade bundles and a verifiable upgrade graph so operators can perform safe roll-forwards...
text: This PR adds a CI-first pipeline to produce deterministic delta upgrade packages and stamp SBOM/provenance metadata into the private-installer release artifacts. Motivation: customers running Private (VPC/on-prem) need compact upgrade bundles and a verifiable upgrade graph so operators can perform safe roll-forwards and rollbacks without re-pulling full images. The output of the pipeline is a signed delta-index (delta-index.json) that expresses available upgrade paths between installer versions and references per-artifact SBOMs, Helm chart lockfiles, and Terraform module plan snapshots. Changes include: 1) new pipeline job (private-installer:delta-pack) that builds compact tarballs containing only changed manifests and image digests; 2) delta-indexer tool (tools/release/delta-indexer.go) which computes graph edges and emits SBOM references; 3) SBOM stamping step using syft and a light...

title: weighted-queue-and-kv-pin-timeouts-to-mitigate-starvation
preview: Context: Production traces and perf canaries exposed recurring tail-latency regressions under mixed workloads with hot and cold tenants. Root cause analysis showed that the continuous batching scheduler's FIFO-dominant queue allowed small high-priority requests to be repeatedly overtaken when the runtime favored lar...
text: Context: Production traces and perf canaries exposed recurring tail-latency regressions under mixed workloads with hot and cold tenants. Root cause analysis showed that the continuous batching scheduler's FIFO-dominant queue allowed small high-priority requests to be repeatedly overtaken when the runtime favored large batched prefixes; simultaneously the KV cache had a long pin expiry that amplified hot-key monopolization. This PR introduces two coordinated changes: 1) a lightweight weighted-queue throttler in the continuous batcher that enforces service-share weights at queue admission time (soft scheduling), and 2) adjustable KV-cache pin timeouts and a small-metadata eviction hint to reduce long-running pin monopolies. Design: - Admissions now record a per-queue weight token and a sliding-window deficit counter; long-running peers lose admission weight over configurable windows to ...

title: fp8-autotune-dequant-rewrite-and-kernel-router
preview: Motivation: We observed cache-sensitive and sequence-length-dependent regressions when serving FP8/INT8 model variants on the high-throughput path. Two root causes surfaced in profiling: (1) redundant dequantization stubs inserted by the compiler leading to extra memory traffic across the KV cache boundary, and (2) ...
text:
Motivation: We observed cache-sensitive and sequence-length-dependent regressions when serving FP8/INT8 model variants on the high-throughput path. Two root causes surfaced in profiling: (1) redundant dequantization stubs inserted by the compiler leading to extra memory traffic across the KV cache boundary, and (2) suboptimal kernel selection for mixed FP8/INT8 attention matmuls at mid-sequence lengths, which increases both latency tail and per-token GPU utilization. This PR introduces an autotuned FP8 dequant rewrite pass (dequant-rewriter) plus a scheduler-aware kernel router that picks kernels based on runtime signals (sequence position, KV cache hit pattern, and batching profile).

Summary of changes:
- Implement dequant-rewriter: a graph-level pass that coalesces adjacent dequant ops, hoists dequantization out of hot KV cache paths when safe, and emits a fused "dequant+transpose"...

title: Dependency-aware rollout orchestrator with safety chains and cohort quarantine
preview: Motivation: We frequently run multi-model experiments (A/B tests, shadow+canary hybrids) where some models are dependent on upstream feature transformations or other model variants. Current rollouts treat each variant independently which can lead to invalid traffic splits and unsafe rollbacks when dependent cohorts ...
text:
Motivation: We frequently run multi-model experiments (A/B tests, shadow+canary hybrids) where some models are dependent on upstream feature transformations or other model variants. Current rollouts treat each variant independently which can lead to invalid traffic splits and unsafe rollbacks when dependent cohorts are promoted or quarantined. This PR introduces a dependency-aware rollout orchestrator that models 'safety chains' between rollout nodes so that promotion/rollback decisions are propagated deterministically across related cohorts.

Summary of changes: Adds a new orchestration component (dependency orchestrator) to the servegraph pipeline, implements safety-chain semantics, and extends the rollout policy language with two new directives: "requires" (declare dependencies) and "quarantine_on_failure" (automatic cohort isolation). The orchestrator supports: 1) transactional co...

title: openai-v1-chat-params-clarity-and-toolcall-debounce-for-stream-stability
preview: Motivation: We continue to close the gap between Redwood's /v1/chat compatibility surface and the OpenAI chat/completions expectations. Customers reported two classes of issues: (1) noisy rapid fncall footprints during network retries and websocket reconnects that caused multiple tool invocations, and (2) ambiguous ...
text:
Motivation: We continue to close the gap between Redwood's /v1/chat compatibility surface and the OpenAI chat/completions expectations. Customers reported two classes of issues: (1) noisy rapid fncall footprints during network retries and websocket reconnects that caused multiple tool invocations, and (2) ambiguous behavior when multiple stop signals, role aliases, or logprob fallbacks co-occurred in streaming output. This PR implements two coordinated changes: (A) a parameter clarity layer that normalizes and documents v1 chat params (role aliases, stop token semantics, logprob backoff) so downstream stages get a deterministic shape; (B) a lightweight toolcall debounce and stream heartbeat handshake that prevents duplicated function/tool invocations on transient reconnects and provides a clearer stream parity signal to clients. 

What changed (high level):
- Added a v1 param canonica...

title: Invoke compatibility proto mapper and decision telemetry
preview: Motivation: Customers expect OpenAI-style function/tool calling to 'just work' when routed through Redwood's serving runtime. However, in practice we see a set of recurring issues: heterogeneous tool descriptors from third-party models, ambiguous tool_choice semantics when multiple tools match, and stream fragmentat...
text: Motivation: Customers expect OpenAI-style function/tool calling to 'just work' when routed through Redwood's serving runtime. However, in practice we see a set of recurring issues: heterogeneous tool descriptors from third-party models, ambiguous tool_choice semantics when multiple tools match, and stream fragmentation that splits the tool invocation payload across SSE boundaries. This PR introduces a compatibility-focused proto mapper and decision telemetry that together make tool invocation more deterministic and observable.Design summary: 1) proto_mapper: a lightweight rule engine that canonicalizes incoming OpenAI-compatible invocation envelopes into a single Redwood internal representation. It normalizes fields (name/description/schema/arguments), expands legacy aliases into canonical redwood_tool_id, and attaches a compat_hint when the mapping required heuristics. Mappings are d...

title: tooling/execution: concurrency safeguards and tool-result-injection ergonomics
preview: Motivation: Customers integrating complex tool chains reported flaky outputs when multiple tools ran in parallel and tool results were injected back into the model stream. This PR standardizes execution semantics for multi-tool calls, adds runtime safeguards for parallelism, and harmonizes behavior with our OpenAI-c...
text: Motivation: Customers integrating complex tool chains reported flaky outputs when multiple tools ran in parallel and tool results were injected back into the model stream. This PR standardizes execution semantics for multi-tool calls, adds runtime safeguards for parallelism, and harmonizes behavior with our OpenAI-compat surface. Summary of changes: 1) Executor: introduce a per-request "tool-parallelism" cap (configurable at API/route level) that bounds concurrent tool invocations and provides backpressure hooks to the scheduler; 2) Injection protocol: formalize a deterministic injection ordering when multiple tool results arrive within the same model turn and attach an idempotency token to each injected result so retries do not duplicate content; 3) Retry semantics: new retry policy enum (none, best-effort, strict-idempotent) exposed via the API and respected by the runtime and OpenA...

title: introduce-budget-mapper-mini-batcher-defaults-and-lightweight-quant-profiles
preview: Motivation: many tenants operate with low to moderate QPS where full tenant-wide prefill and large batch policies add cost without improving tail latency. This PR adds a lightweight 'budget mapper' that emits per-tenant mini-batcher defaults and an opinionated set of small quantization profiles so teams can get cost...
text:
Motivation: many tenants operate with low to moderate QPS where full tenant-wide prefill and large batch policies add cost without improving tail latency. This PR adds a lightweight 'budget mapper' that emits per-tenant mini-batcher defaults and an opinionated set of small quantization profiles so teams can get cost-down defaults that are safe for production.

Summary of changes: adds a BudgetMapper component that infers conservative mini-batching and cache window defaults from historical unit-econ signals (p95 latency, tokens-per-request, cold-start rate). Introduces a tiny, validated quant_profiles.yaml with three compact profiles (tiny-int8, lean-int4, compat-fp16) and a CLI subcommand 'redwood econ inspect' to preview tenant defaults. Defaults are intentionally conservative and include automatic safe fallbacks (no quantization for models flagged as unsafe). The PR wires telemetry ...

title: Reduce CPU serialization overhead and introduce request-affine thread pinning
preview: Motivation: production canaries showed CPU-side stalls in the request path under medium tail-load (concurrent short requests). Root cause analysis indicated three correlated issues: a global serialization lock in the request marshalling path, KV-cache read contention on a small number of shards, and excessive cross-...
text:
Motivation: production canaries showed CPU-side stalls in the request path under medium tail-load (concurrent short requests). Root cause analysis indicated three correlated issues: a global serialization lock in the request marshalling path, KV-cache read contention on a small number of shards, and excessive cross-thread wakeups from the executor when continuous batching is active. This PR addresses those CPU-side bottlenecks with low-risk, togglable changes that reduce serialization overhead and make scheduling request-affine so hot requests avoid context migration.

Summary of changes: I) Replace global request-serialization mutex with per-worker small-buffer fast-path using FlatBuffers for marshalling and a fallback to proto when necessary. II) Introduce a request-affine thread-pool (request-affine executors) that pins a request's lifecycle to a CPU core group to reduce cache chur...

title: introduce-lightweight-local-dev-runner-and-toolchain-stub
preview: Context and motivation: onboarding and fast feedback for local CI iterations has regressed as our CI matrix grew. This PR introduces a lightweight local dev runner (tools/fastdev) and a minimal toolchain stub (dev/Toolchain.toml) plus a small workflow that developers can run locally or in ephemeral self-hosted runne...
text: Context and motivation: onboarding and fast feedback for local CI iterations has regressed as our CI matrix grew. This PR introduces a lightweight local dev runner (tools/fastdev) and a minimal toolchain stub (dev/Toolchain.toml) plus a small workflow that developers can run locally or in ephemeral self-hosted runners to get the same sanity checks as CI without the full matrix. This is an opt-in developer convenience feature that reduces time-to-first-green and iteration time on lint/test changes. Summary of changes: - add tools/fastdev/runner.go: small Go binary that runs a subset of checks in-process with conservative timeouts and filesystem caching - add tools/fastdev/README.md: usage, examples, and how to wire into VS Code tasks - add dev/Toolchain.toml: lightweight list of pinned tool versions for local dev (go, node, python, prettier, husky minimal config) - add .github/workflow...

title: add kernel-hotspot tracer, kvcache offset histogram, and continuous batch pressure probe
preview: Motivation: It has been difficult to correlate kernel-level hotspots with KV cache layout and continuous-batching backpressure events in production traces. This PR introduces three complementary low-overhead probes and a small microbench harness to make cause-and-effect visible during perf investigations and automat...
text: Motivation: It has been difficult to correlate kernel-level hotspots with KV cache layout and continuous-batching backpressure events in production traces. This PR introduces three complementary low-overhead probes and a small microbench harness to make cause-and-effect visible during perf investigations and automated regressions. Summary of changes: (1) kernel-hotspot-tracer: lightweight sampling probe that annotates scheduler and CUDA kernel entry/exit with a compact hotspot identifier derived from callsite and op-type. Emits NVTX ranges when available and falls back to short tracepoints for environments without NVTX. Includes FLOPS-adjusted counters to avoid over-weighting long-running kernels. (2) kvcache-offset-histogram: on-KVcache-access histogram recording offset distribution and small-window reuse counts to detect pathological stride patterns introduced by certain tokenizers ...

title: per-channel requantization + kernel fusion with KV-cache hinting to improve INT8/FP8 steady-state throughput
preview:
Title: Per-channel requantization + kernel fusion with KV-cache hinting to improve INT8/FP8 steady-state throughput

Summary / Motivation:
We observed that for real traffic profiles (small interactive requests with hot prefixes) the current INT8 execution path suffers from uneven per-head/per-channel scaling which r...
text:
Title: Per-channel requantization + kernel fusion with KV-cache hinting to improve INT8/FP8 steady-state throughput

Summary / Motivation:
We observed that for real traffic profiles (small interactive requests with hot prefixes) the current INT8 execution path suffers from uneven per-head/per-channel scaling which results in lower GEMM utilization and extra dequant/quant roundtrips around attention and MLP. This PR introduces a per-channel requantization stage inside the microkernel and fuses the dequant/scale operations into the attention + MLP kernels when safe. Additionally, it adds a lightweight KV-cache hint that allows the scheduler to prefer pre-warmed KV workers when requantization is detected, reducing cold-KV stalls for hot prefixes. The goal is to increase steady-state throughput without impacting worst-case correctness or model quality.

What changed (high level):
- Introd...

title: Cost Model API: add policy guardrails for what-if projections (bounds + allowed ranges)
preview: Context: The new Cost Model what-if endpoint is intentionally flexible (batching/caching/quantization knobs) and will be called by Optimize (interactive) and potentially Console (hot paths). In early staging tests and after SUP-31877 triage, we saw two failure modes: (1) callers passing extreme parameter values (e.g...
text: Context: The new Cost Model what-if endpoint is intentionally flexible (batching/caching/quantization knobs) and will be called by Optimize (interactive) and potentially Console (hot paths). In early staging tests and after SUP-31877 triage, we saw two failure modes: (1) callers passing extreme parameter values (e.g., batch size 4096, cache warm-rate > 1.0) leading to unrealistic savings outputs, and (2) expensive computations when callers submit high-cardinality workload summaries (large histograms / too many route keys) that can blow our latency budget and increase tail p95s. This PR adds explicit policy guardrails at the API boundary so the service is safe-by-default, deterministic, and bounded. Summary of changes: (A) Introduce a centralized PolicyGuardrails layer for what-if requests that validates and clamps input ranges (where clamping is explicitly allowed) and rejects invalid...

title: Unify OpenAI Responses v8: seed routing + context-tag metadata passthrough
preview: Summary: Add a compatibility layer for the incoming Responses v8 variant that preserves deterministic hints (seed/rng flags), forwards client metadata tags intact, and stabilizes streaming chunk boundaries for tool/function call reassembly. This PR centralizes the mapping logic so that other adapters (SDKs, private ...
text:
Summary: Add a compatibility layer for the incoming Responses v8 variant that preserves deterministic hints (seed/rng flags), forwards client metadata tags intact, and stabilizes streaming chunk boundaries for tool/function call reassembly. This PR centralizes the mapping logic so that other adapters (SDKs, private installer) can reuse the same translation rules. Motivation: Customers are adopting the new Responses v8 semantics (explicit seed + context-tag fields). Without a consistent mapping we see reproducibility regressions and toolcall reassembly bugs in streaming. This change reduces conversion drift and eliminates a class of flakiness observed in production canaries.

What this changes (high-level):
- Add responses-v8 adapter in pkg/openai/compat that maps v8 flags to internal RNG hints (seed-channel + deterministic-level).
- Preserve top-level client metadata (user_metadata, c...

title: Introduce invocation affinity matrix and proactive alert resolver
preview: Motivation: We observe recurring noisy SLO alerts created by correlated bursts that cross tenant thresholds without reflecting distinct root causes. Current per-invocation alerts fire independently and cause paging fatigue. This PR adds a first-class invocation affinity metric and a proactive alert resolver that gro...
text:
Motivation: We observe recurring noisy SLO alerts created by correlated bursts that cross tenant thresholds without reflecting distinct root causes. Current per-invocation alerts fire independently and cause paging fatigue. This PR adds a first-class invocation affinity metric and a proactive alert resolver that groups related invocations into cohorts and suppresses/attenuates alerts when an upstream affinity signal indicates a single shared cause.

What this PR does: Adds a streaming affinity index computed at aggregation time (invocation_affinity{route, model, tenant, window}) derived from co-occurrence of invocation fingerprints, kernel contention signals, and token-phase overlap. Adds a Grafana panel to visualize the affinity matrix and an alert resolver that consumes the affinity index to decide alert coalescing and attenuation thresholds. The resolver is opt-in and governed by p...
